content.text:
Recent discussions on X (formerly Twitter) about **research article scraping**, **AI-powered web search**, **article scraping**, and related tools highlight a growing ecosystem of LLM-native scraping solutions, agentic RAG systems, and specialized APIs for researchers and developers.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019061395342622947)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018912401262014676)

### Key Tools and Projects Mentioned:
- **AnyCrawl**: An open-source LLM-native web scraping tool (2.5k stars) where you describe what you want, and AI extracts structured data without selectors. Contrasts with traditional anti-bot methods.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018912401262014676)
- **Desearch.ai**: AI-powered search and scraping API (16x more affordable), optimized for real-time relevance in dynamic environments like news scraping. Built for devs and AI agents.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018627541104558189)
- **ScrapeGraphAI**: LLM-driven web scraping suite for extracting structured data from websites/HTML, now integrated with agentic payment APIs via Orthogonal (YC W26).[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2017428603684982973)
- **SerpApi**: Real-time API for scraping Google Search results (organic, news, images, etc.) in JSON. Includes "Google Light Search API" for fast critical data extraction.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2019169119547859017)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2018226399547633742)
- **A-RAG**: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework. Lets models autonomously choose tools like keyword_search, semantic_search, or chunk_read for hierarchical retrieval. Outperforms baselines on benchmarks like HotpotQA (94.5%).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019061395342622947)

### AI Workflows for Research Papers:
- Researchers recommend Perplexity (Academic mode + Deep Research) → NotebookLM (for lit reviews, tables, audio) → ChatGPT for verification. Avoids tab overload.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2017591194826440879)
- Tools for chatting with papers: "Chat with Research Papers" AI for explanations/inferences from pasted sections.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2018602867444363633)
- Finding research gaps: Elicit, ResearchRabbit, ConnectedPapers, Litmaps, Scite.ai.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2018609923492598268)

Other niches include web/data scraping for rural data gaps[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2019004097953767493) and general web scraping practice abstracts.

No top-ranked posts matched your exact keyword query, but these semantically relevant recent posts (mostly Feb 2026) show active interest. For more, I can dive into specific threads or images/videos!
